matches.match_rationale: Branch teachers in niche areas (Special Ed, GT, STEM) lack access to domain-specific onboarding materials and training content. AI text generation can produce tailored professional development curricula, specialist onboarding guides, and role-specific training modules at scale — replacing the HQ-centric model where only in-person cohorts receive this content. This directly addresses the 'no online specialist resources' gap.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A content generation system that produces campus-ready PD modules for specialist roles — e.g., a 10-part Special Ed induction curriculum or STEM instructional method guides — deployable to any branch without HQ involvement.

matches.match_rationale: Branch staff have no access to specialist expertise or knowledge repositories. A RAG system over curated specialist PD content (pedagogy docs, frameworks, research) lets any teacher at any campus query domain-specific knowledge and receive grounded, cited guidance — effectively distributing HQ specialist knowledge to branches on demand. This is the core technical fix for 'no online specialist resources' and addresses quality variance.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A specialist knowledge assistant where a branch Special Ed teacher can ask 'How do I implement a behavior support plan for a non-verbal student?' and receive answers grounded in the organization's approved frameworks and external research — available 24/7 from any campus.

matches.match_rationale: New specialist teachers need ongoing, adaptive domain coaching — not just static modules. A conversational AI tutor can guide a new STEM or GT teacher through instructional methods, answer follow-up questions, and adapt to their specific classroom challenges over time. This directly replaces the mentorship and coaching that only HQ-proximate staff currently receive from senior specialists.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI coaching agent for specialist role onboarding — a new Gifted & Talented teacher at a rural branch works through an interactive dialogue-based orientation covering GT identification methods, differentiated instruction, and IEP documentation, with follow-up Q&A between formal PD sessions.

matches.match_rationale: Without a recommendation layer, branch teachers don't know what specialist PD resources exist or which are most relevant to their role and development stage. A recommendation engine can proactively surface the most relevant PD content to each teacher based on their role, campus context, and learning history — closing the equity gap where only HQ staff have a curator (their nearby specialist colleagues) guiding their development.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A personalized PD recommendation feed inside the LMS that surfaces specialist articles, micro-courses, and peer case studies to each teacher based on their subject area, certification gaps, and recent lesson plans — replacing the informal HQ hallway knowledge transfer with an algorithmic equity lever.

matches.match_rationale: The 'weak specialist pipeline' problem means the org fails to identify and develop potential specialist teachers before roles go unfilled. A propensity model can score current teachers on likelihood to succeed in specialist roles (based on credentials, PD completion, student outcomes, tenure) — enabling proactive pipeline investment at branches rather than reactive HQ-led recruitment. This targets the 'specialist roles unfilled because pipeline is weak' mechanism.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A talent pipeline model that flags branch teachers with high specialist potential 12-18 months before predicted role openings — enabling targeted scholarship, mentorship, or PD investment so vacancies are filled from within rather than left unfilled.
